Key Finding (Phase NS)
timeline.detail: (Ofgem/DESNZ empirical): rising absolute prices do NOT increase switching propensity -- 2022 (bills at GBP3,549 cap, switching at 3-4%) definitively disconfirms the price-momentum hypothesis. Savings available is the primary driver: zero savings -> 44% suppression of switching even for bill-shocked customers; peak comp...

timeline.label: Flexibility Revenue Site/ Dashboard + Annual Report Extension
Key Finding (Phase PQ)
timeline.detail: (live 2016-2025 run): overall_rag=RED -- 2022 normalised churn propensity 2.4x 2020 propensity (22.2% / 0.44x vs 20% / 0.95x), indicating the market switching multiplier is not fully suppressing I&C churn in crisis years. Small-N caveat (9 renewals in 2022) documented. Priority 3 complete. Epistemic: PASS.

timeline.label: Key Finding (Phase PR)
timeline.detail: SIM long-run average churn 6.4% vs Ofgem 13.6% (ratio=0.47, GREEN) -- SIM is BELOW market average over the full decade; the Phase PQ RED flag was false alarm from single-year small-N noise (2 churns in 9 renewals). 2022 single-year rate still 5.6x Ofgem but rolling 3-year criterion not met. overall_rag AMBER (bad debt ...

timeline.label: Key Finding (Phase PS)
timeline.detail: Complaint rate (4-6% per billing period) GREEN in all years except 2025 (AMBER at 6.1%). Arrears elevated in most years (30% 2016, 29% 2022); only 2020 GREEN (5.3%) when I&C-dominated and stable -- surfaces that arrears model calibrated towards residential rates, not pure I&C. timeline.label: Key Finding (Phase PU)
timeline.detail: as-of 2025-06-07, C9 in renewal window (22 days), proposed 153.49 vs current 210.0 GBP/MWh (market fallen from 2022 peak); hedge rec INCREASE (I&C pass-through at 0% hf -- expected). SIM no longer retrodiction-only. P4 MVP delivered.

timeline.label: Key Finding (Phase PW)
timeline.detail: IC arrears 0% -> 5.4% (10-year aggregate); all years rag=GREEN. Previous PS finding (29-31% RED) was comparing full portfolio (resi income-stress arrears) to I&C commercial benchmark. Phase PW closes calibration gap: arrears_vs_benchmark now separates corporate credit risk from household income stress. Epistemic: PASS.
